notes: 1. RCBO หมายถึง เซอร์กิตเบรกเกอร์ชนิดมีเครื่องตัดไฟรั่วในตัว
warnings: This is a single-line/panel schedule diagram rather than a tabular schedule -- circuit_no/breaker/wire/description fields used to fit the data cleanly, same convention as house #1/#2's equivalent sheet.

This panel design differs from house #1/#2's (which served a single-story house with 1 lighting + 1 receptacle + 1 WH circuit, 6 active positions total including main): this is a 6-circuit-active 10-way panel with the lighting/receptacle/WH circuits doubled -- one of each pair per floor (odd numbers 1/3/5 = ground floor, even numbers 2/4/6 = upper floor), which was confirmed by cross-referencing each circuit's wire spec and description against the '/LP' circuit tags actually printed on EE-03 through EE-06.

The printed circuit-number row under the breakers reads '1 2 3 4 [gap] 6 7 8 9 10' -- the numeral '5' itself is faint/not clearly legible in the source image (likely obscured near the WH graphic), but a 6th active breaker position clearly exists between position 4 and the visible '6' (matching the 6th wire-drop/description label '2-4/G2.5...' + 'กล่องต่อสายเครื่องทำน้ำอุ่น'), so it has been read as circuit 5 by position and by matching against the '5/LP' tag seen on EE-05. Flagged as the single lowest-confidence read on this sheet.
Load wattage (load_watts) is not printed anywhere on this sheet, so it is omitted rather than estimated/fabricated.

Service/panel spec (kWh meter rating, main breaker 50AT 2P IC10kA, ground rod size ø14.2mm/2.40m, RCBO note) is verbatim-identical to house #1/#2's EE-02 -- same standard 1-phase residential LP panel design reused across the drawing family, just with more active circuits here to serve 2 floors.
No grid/floor-plan content on this sheet -- it is purely the panel single-line diagram.
